patches.find: The refraction cascade (Section 1.8) adds a further constraint: at the Antarctic rim (r ≈ 15,500–20,015 km), the firmament ceiling H(r) ≈ 1,400–840 km.
patches.replace: The refraction cascade (Section 1.8) adds a further constraint: at the Antarctic rim (r ≈ 15,500–20,015 km), the firmament ceiling H(r) ≈ 1,400–840 km — a 6–10× collapse from the polar value H(0) = 8,537 km, so any monotonic H↔g coupling consistent with the dome’s own κ-mediated framework forces a 5–10×-class gravity drop across the same arc on which WGS84 measures ≤0.063% variation. The dome cannot rescue the prediction by reinterpreting H(r) as ‘apparent rather than physical’ (the standard refraction-fix escape) without simultaneously invalidating the Polaris-elevation calibration from which both r and H(r) were derived — Section 1.8 closes that exit. Either H(r) is physical and the gravity prediction overshoots by 170–1,430×, or H(r) is apparent and there is no dome-native r at which to evaluate the prediction at all.
patches.rationale: EXP-246: complete the hanging closing sentence (ISS-1503 / curmudgeon c10 H4). Replaces stub sentence that announced a constraint but never delivered it. Three-sentence synthesis: (1) quantifies H(r) collapse 6-10x from pole to rim, (2) derives 5-10x-class gravity drop under any monotonic H<->g coupling versus WGS84 0.063% measured variation, (3) preempts apparent-vs-physical escape via Section 1.8 coordinate-collapse argument. Also upgrades anchor from #part1 to #section-1-8.
